name: Pico Peñarroya - Alto de la Gitana Loop Trail
name_original: Mirador de Peñarroya – circular por la Senda del Maestrazgo (GR 8)
description: This moderate 2.7-mile (4.4 km) jogging loop in Sierra de Gúdar offers challenging terrain and panoramic views from Mirador de Peñarroya.
region_intro: Jogging around Valdelinares offers routes through the high-altitude Sierra de Gúdar mountains, known for their diverse natural features. The region is characterized by dense pine forests, open mountain terrain, and significant peaks like Peñarroya Summit, which rises over 2000 meters. This varied landscape provides a range of running trails suitable for different fitness levels, from shaded forest paths to more challenging mountain ascents. Best jogging routes around Valdelinares The most popular jogging route is Pico Peñarroya - Alto de la Gitana Loop Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) moderate trail that takes about 40 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the surrounding mountain landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Valdelinares, a difficult 8.0 miles (12.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through varied mountain terrain. Local runners also love the Valdelinares Ski Resort – Peñarroya Summit Viewpoint loop from Valdelinares, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail leading through high mountain areas and past the ski resort, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Jogging around Valdelinares is defined by high mountain peaks, dense pine forests, and open mountain terrain. The extensive network of running routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult long-distance challenges. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 90 runners have used komoot to explore Valdelinares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Valdelinares?
A: Valdelinares offers a selection of 18 dedicated running routes, catering to various fitness levels. These trails wind through the high-altitude Sierra de Gúdar mountains, providing diverse landscapes for your run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Valdelinares?
A: The terrain around Valdelinares is quite varied, characterized by high-altitude mountain paths, dense pine forests (including black pine populations), and open mountain landscapes. You'll encounter everything from shaded forest trails to more challenging ascents towards peaks like Peñarroya Summit, which offers panoramic views.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Valdelinares?
A: While many routes in Valdelinares feature significant elevation changes, there are options suitable for moderate fitness levels. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983372781/pico-penarroya-alto-de-la-gitana-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Peñarroya - Alto de la Gitana Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 2.7-mile (4.4 km) route that takes about 40 minutes to complete, offering scenic mountain views without extreme difficulty.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I can see while jogging in Valdelinares?
A: Many running routes offer stunning views and pass by notable landmarks. You can enjoy panoramic vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5805644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Summit and Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt;, or incorporate cultural points of interest like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva Castle&lt;/a&gt; into your longer runs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelinares Ski Resort&lt;/a&gt; area also offers unique landscapes when not covered in snow.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Valdelinares area?
A: Yes, Valdelinares offers several circular running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983372781/pico-penarroya-alto-de-la-gitana-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Peñarroya - Alto de la Gitana Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;. For a more challenging option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelinares Ski Resort – Peñarroya Summit Viewpoint loop from Valdelinares&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15.5-mile (25 km) trail that forms a complete loop.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Valdelinares?
A: Valdelinares is excellent for jogging during the warmer months, typically from late spring through early autumn. Its high altitude provides cooler temperatures, making it a refreshing escape from the heat. While known for its ski resort, summer transforms the area into a prime destination for foot-based activities.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families in Valdelinares?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, some moderate trails can be enjoyed by active families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983372781/pico-penarroya-alto-de-la-gitana-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Peñarroya - Alto de la Gitana Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, moderate option that might be suitable for families looking for a scenic outing.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Valdelinares?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural areas like Valdelinares, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations or specific trail signage, particularly within protected areas or near livestock, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: Are there water sources available along the running trails?
A: The Sierra de Gúdar region surrounding Valdelinares features numerous natural springs and fountains, such as El Vallejo or Fuente del Nacimiento. These can serve as natural hydration points during longer runs, but it's always recommended to carry sufficient water, especially on less-traveled paths.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running routes in Valdelinares?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Valdelinares, as well as at popular trailheads or points of interest like the Valdelinares Ski Resort. Specific parking information for individual routes can often be found on komoot tour pages or local signage.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Valdelinares?
A: The komoot community frequently praises Valdelinares for its diverse and challenging mountain terrain, the refreshing high-altitude environment, and the stunning panoramic views from peaks like Peñarroya. The extensive network of trails, from dense pine forests to open mountain landscapes, offers a rewarding experience for all levels of runners.

Q: Can I combine my run with a visit to a nearby village or attraction?
A: Absolutely! Many routes are designed to pass through or near charming mountain villages and historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva – Alcalá de la Selva Castle loop from Urbanización Solano de la Vega&lt;/a&gt; allows you to explore the area around the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva Castle&lt;/a&gt; as part of your run.
tips: Keep your eyes wide open so as not to miss any landscape

faqs.question: Where does the Pico Peñarroya - Alto de la Gitana Loop Trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: A common and convenient starting point for this loop trail is the Collado de la Gitana. You'll often find ample parking available there, making it an ideal spot to begin your adventure.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Pico Peñarroya - Alto de la Gitana Loop Trail for jogging?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate in difficulty for hiking, but for continuous jogging, it presents a significant challenge. You'll encounter diverse terrain, including strong inclines and steep descents, sections without a clear path, and varied surfaces from pine forest floors to rocky areas. It's best suited for experienced trail runners seeking a demanding workout with technical sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a very diverse and often demanding terrain. You'll navigate well-defined paths and wider tracks, but also sections without a clear path that require careful navigation, sometimes guided by cairns. Expect strong inclines, particularly towards the peak, and steep descents. The ground varies from soft pine forest floors to grassy slopes and rocky areas, including ancient stone walls and a firebreak.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is reaching Peñarroya Summit and Observation Tower, the highest point in Teruel. While the summit itself can be tree-covered, the nearby Mirador de Peñarroya offers breathtaking 360-degree panoramic views of the Sierra de Gúdar. You'll also find a distinctive geodetic vertex at the summit and may pass the recreational area of Fuente de la Chaparrilla.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this loop trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is best enjoyed during the warmer months when conditions are generally more favorable. During winter, snow and ice can transform the route into exacting alpine terrain, making it suitable only for experts with specialized equipment like crampons. For jogging, aim for spring, summer, or autumn to avoid these challenging winter conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Peñarroya - Alto de la Gitana Loop Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the varied and sometimes challenging terrain, including steep sections and areas without a clear path, ensure your dog is fit for such conditions and always keep them on a leash for their safety and to respect local wildlife and other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Sierra de Gúdar area?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit or have to pay an entrance fee to access the Pico Peñarroya - Alto de la Gitana Loop Trail within the Sierra de Gúdar. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other long-distance paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, portions of this loop trail follow established routes. You will find sections that coincide with the GR 8 long-distance path, as well as parts of the MontañasVacías, Senda del Maestrazgo, and Pista a Pico Peñarroya trails.

faqs.question: What are the conditions like on the trail during winter?
faqs.answer: During winter, the trail's character changes significantly. Snow and ice are common, transforming the route into exacting alpine terrain. It becomes suitable only for experienced individuals equipped with appropriate gear such as crampons and ice axes. It is not recommended for jogging during these months.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back?
faqs.answer: As the name suggests, the Pico Peñarroya - Alto de la Gitana Loop Trail is a circular route. You will start and finish at the same point, offering a varied experience without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like water sources or refuges along the way?
faqs.answer: While the trail is primarily a natural experience, you may come across the recreational area of Fuente de la Chaparrilla. This spot features a spring and a refuge, which could be a useful point for a break or to refill water, depending on the season and water availability.
